trace.text:
Your **0–9 button counter** is built and running! Here's everything you need to know:

---

## 🔌 Wiring Table

| Arduino Pin | Component | Function |
|-------------|-----------|----------|
| **D2** | 7-Seg pin **a** | Top segment |
| **D3** | 7-Seg pin **b** | Upper-right segment |
| **D4** | 7-Seg pin **c** | Lower-right segment |
| **D5** | 7-Seg pin **d** | Bottom segment |
| **D6** | 7-Seg pin **e** | Lower-left segment |
| **D7** | 7-Seg pin **f** | Upper-left segment |
| **D8** | 7-Seg pin **g** | Middle segment |
| **GND** | 7-Seg **common** | Common cathode ground |
| **D9** | Button **leg A** | Button input (INPUT_PULLUP) |
| **GND** | Button **leg B** | Button ground |

> Seven 220Ω resistors (R_A through R_G) are wired in series with each segment to limit current.

---

## 📟 How It Works

- **Button press detected** via falling-edge detection (HIGH→LOW) with `INPUT_PULLUP` — no external pull-up resistor needed.
- A **50ms debounce delay** prevents multiple counts per press.
- `showDigit(n)` drives all 7 segments using a plain `if/else` chain — one digit at a time, no arrays.
- After **9**, the next press wraps back to **0**.

---

## ⚡ Power Budget

| Item | Current per segment | Max active segments |
|------|-------------------|-------------------|
| Each lit segment | (5V − 2V) / 220Ω ≈ **~13.6 mA** | 7 (digit "8") |
| **Worst case (digit 8)** | 7 × 13.6 mA = **~95 mA** total | Well within Uno 500mA USB limit |
| Per Arduino pin | **≤ 13.6 mA** | ✅ Under 20mA limit |
| Button pin | **~0 mA** (INPUT_PULLUP) | ✅ Safe |

**All pins are within the safe 20mA per-pin limit.** Total draw is well within the Arduino Uno's budget. 🟢
